headline: Maria is stuck on a too-vague last-cycle readout, and it’s now leaking into clarity, rituals, and her ability to define growth decisions.
summary_bullets: Maria’s blocker is specificity: “The feedback was not specific enough” and she “want[s] clearer scope,” so she can’t convert the decision into next actions.
Her growth path is currently parked in mentoring: she says “Mentoring matters to me” and wants “one mentoring commitment that has clear scope,” but she can’t name what would “move the next decision.”
Engagement is declining in rituals for a concrete reason: “I am less motivated in team rituals lately,” tied to wanting “impact at org level, not just strong project work.”
understanding_paragraph: Maria is not avoiding the last-cycle outcome; she’s still processing it because the feedback lacked actionable detail. She wants org-level impact, but she can’t yet articulate the decision criteria that would change her trajectory. The clearest next step she can commit to is a scoped mentoring commitment with a review point next month.

axes.meaning: A mild but real dip shows up in ritual motivation, suggesting stress/energy drag tied to unresolved decision processing.
Engagement is weak in team rituals lately, even though mentoring still matters to her.
Clarity is low because the last-cycle feedback lacked specificity and she still needs clearer scope.
Growth definition is blocked: she cannot name what would “move the next decision,” so trajectory planning is stalled.

brutal_truth_employee: Maria is stuck on the missing specificity from last cycle: “The feedback was not specific enough” and “I want clearer scope.” She also can’t define the growth decision she needs: “I do not know what would move the next decision.”
brutal_truth_manager: When Maria said “The feedback was not specific enough” and then “I want clearer scope,” the conversation still didn’t land on the exact scope elements she needs (outcomes, evidence, and who owns the readout). The moment to tighten the decision path was when she admitted “I do not know what would move the next decision” and then pivoted to mentoring without naming the decision criteria.

next_actions.action: Email Maria the exact last-cycle decision readout you have (gap, evidence, and what would change the outcome) and ask her to mark what’s still missing.
Set up a 30-minute scope-workshop with Maria to define her mentoring commitment: 1 org-level outcome, 1 evidence artifact, and a review checkpoint next month.
Start the next 1:1 by asking Maria to name the decision criteria she needs (“what would move the next decision?”) and force one concrete org-level impact target.

watch_for: Before next 1:1: if Maria still can’t state the decision criteria (“what would move the next decision”), treat growth as blocked, not just delayed.
Within two weeks: if her ritual motivation stays low after the mentoring scope is clarified, the issue is the org-level impact path—not mentoring itself.
